bag of strategies is presented to the author in the form of two choices bag of strategy a offers a forty percent chance of winning one thousand dollars and a sixty percent chance of losing five hundred dollars bag of expected value of strategy a is calculated by multiplying the probability of winning by the amount won and the probability of losing by the amount lost bag of result is four hundred minus three hundred which equals one hundred bag of strategy b offers a sixty percent chance of winning eight hundred dollars and a forty percent chance of losing two hundred dollars bag of expected value of strategy b is calculated similarly by multiplying the respective probabilities and amounts bag of result is four hundred and eighty minus eighty which equals four hundred bag of expected values are compared and strategy b is chosen because it offers a higher expected value
